concept: Kitchen / Place of Food Preparation / Nourishment Space
description: A room where food is prepared and cooked; the space dedicated to nourishment. 'Nuroa' names where food transforms—ingredients becoming meals, raw becoming nourishing.
sound_symbolism: The word nurtures. 'Nu' grounds with sustaining presence, nourishment forming. 'Ro' stirs and transforms. Finally 'a' opens to completion, food ready.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo nuroa' (the kitchen). Related to 'moeli' (hearth)—the kitchen is the modern hearth, center of domestic nourishment.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Kitchen work is care made visible—the labor of feeding others as expression of love.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Kitchens sustained life—where food was transformed from ingredient to nourishment through skill and care.
